texto: How do you judge the professionals you patronize? Too many people judge them by display factors. Extra points are given to those who wear expensive clothes, drive luxury automobiles, and live in exclusive neighborhoods. They assume a professional is likely to be mediocre, even incompetent, if he lives in a modest home and drives a three-year-old Ford Crown Victoria. Very, very few people judge the quality of the professionals they use by net worth criteria. Many professionals have told us they must look successful to convince their customers/clients that they are.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Thomas J. Stanley que provoca esforço, persistência e resultados. Ao questionar “How do you judge the professionals you patronize? Too many people judge them by display factors. …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre sucesso, societal-expectations, wealth, com foco em wealth.

texto: Before you can become a millionaire, you must learn to think like one. You must learn how to motivate yourself to counter fear with courage. Making critical decisions about your career, business, investments and other resources conjures up fear, fear that is part of the process of becoming a financial success.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Thomas J. Stanley: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “Before you can become a millionaire, you must learn to think like one. You must learn how to moti…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que coragem, success, fear pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de coragem e fear.

texto: Grades don't measure tenacity, courage, leadership, guts or whatever you want to call it. Teachers or any other persons in a position of authority should never tell anybody they will not succeed because they did not get all A's in school.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Thomas J. Stanley sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “Grades don't measure tenacity, courage, leadership, guts or whatever you want to call it. Teacher…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a educacao, coragem, school, útil em momentos de school e want.

texto: Operate your household like a productive business.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Thomas J. Stanley: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “Operate your household like a productive business.” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, wealth-creation, operate pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.
